signal_watch.title: Dry-run deployments with Vercel CLI
signal_watch.author: Vercel AI
signal_watch.summary: You can now preview the framework preset and files that Vercel CLI includes in a deployment before creating one. Run vercel deploy --dry from a linked project: For automation or further inspection, return the complete file manifest as JSON: JSON output includes the detected framework, included and ignored paths, directory size distribution, largest files, file modes, and content hashes. signal_watch.title: Vercel Security Dashboard is in private beta
signal_watch.author: Vercel AI
signal_watch.summary: The Vercel Security Dashboard is now in private beta. It aggregates the security posture of every account and project on Vercel. As teams grow and coding agents make it easy to spin up new projects, small misconfigurations can add up quietly and quickly. Team members without 2FA, publicly accessible preview environments, or long-lived credentials where short-lived ones would do. Each of these can lead to a security incident or breach. The security dashboard flags these findin

signal_watch.title: How to Use RLMs in Deep Agents
signal_watch.author: LangChain
signal_watch.summary: Recursive language models (RLMs) fix context rot by having agents write code that dispatches subagents over context chunks instead of pumping everything in one context window. Deep Agents now implements this through dynamic subagents and a lightweight code interpreter, letting agents programmatically fan out work like grep, map, and reduce over large inputs. We benchmark the approach on OOLONG, a long-context reasoning task, and show it holds up where turn-by-turn agents star
signal_watch.excerpt: Recursive language models (RLMs) fix context rot by having agents write code that dispatches subagents over context chunks instead of pumping everything in one context window. Deep Agents now implements this through dynamic subagents and a lightweight code interpreter, letting agents programmatically fan out work like grep, map, and reduce over large inputs. We benchmark the approach on OOLONG, a long-context reasoning task, and show it holds up where turn-by-turn agents star

signal_watch.title: Fault Tolerance in LangGraph: Retries, Timeouts and Error Handlers
signal_watch.author: LangChain
signal_watch.summary: Production agents fail in ways prototypes never do. This post walks through the three fault tolerance primitives built into LangGraph: RetryPolicy for automatic retries with backoff, TimeoutPolicy for wall-clock and idle-based caps, and error_handler for cleanup logic once retries are exhausted. Learn how they compose, why having them inside the workflow engine matters, and how to use the SAGA pattern to handle multi-step workflows with real-world side effects.
signal_watch.excerpt: Production agents fail in ways prototypes never do. This post walks through the three fault tolerance primitives built into LangGraph: RetryPolicy for automatic retries with backoff, TimeoutPolicy for wall-clock and idle-based caps, and error_handler for cleanup logic once retries are exhausted. Learn how they compose, why having them inside the workflow engine matters, and how to use the SAGA pattern to handle multi-step workflows with real-world side effects.
signal_watch.reason_selected: 当日新抓取且未展示过：Fault Tolerance in LangGraph: Retries, Timeouts and Error Handlers 来自 LangChain 的公开抓取入口，证据链保留原始链接与抓取来源。

hot_rank.title: Fault Tolerance in LangGraph: Retries, Timeouts and Error Handlers
hot_rank.author: LangChain
hot_rank.summary: Production agents fail in ways prototypes never do. This post walks through the three fault tolerance primitives built into LangGraph: RetryPolicy for automatic retries with backoff, TimeoutPolicy for wall-clock and idle-based caps, and error_handler for cleanup logic once retries are exhausted. Learn how they compose, why having them inside the workflow engine matters, and how to use the SAGA pattern to handle multi-step workflows with real-world side effects.
hot_rank.excerpt: Production agents fail in ways prototypes never do. This post walks through the three fault tolerance primitives built into LangGraph: RetryPolicy for automatic retries with backoff, TimeoutPolicy for wall-clock and idle-based caps, and error_handler for cleanup logic once retries are exhausted. Learn how they compose, why having them inside the workflow engine matters, and how to use the SAGA pattern to handle multi-step workflows with real-world side effects.

clusters.title: Dry-run deployments with Vercel CLI
clusters.summary: You can now preview the framework preset and files that Vercel CLI includes in a deployment before creating one. Run vercel deploy --dry from a linked project: For automation or further inspection, return the complete file manifest as JSON: JSON output includes the detected framework, included and ignored paths, directory size distribution, largest files, file modes, and content hashes. Piped and other non-TTY output automatically uses JSON. Agents can use this manifest as a

clusters.title: Vercel Security Dashboard is in private beta
clusters.summary: The Vercel Security Dashboard is now in private beta. It aggregates the security posture of every account and project on Vercel. As teams grow and coding agents make it easy to spin up new projects, small misconfigurations can add up quietly and quickly. Team members without 2FA, publicly accessible preview environments, or long-lived credentials where short-lived ones would do. Each of these can lead to a security incident or breach. The security dashboard flags these findin

clusters.title: 6 security settings every GitHub maintainer should enable this week
clusters.summary: These six free settings will not make your project unhackable. Nothing will. What they will do is close the easy doors. Turn these on, and your project will be meaningfully harder to attack than it was before. The post 6 security settings every GitHub maintainer should enable this week appeared first on The GitHub Blog .

clusters.title: OpenWiki: Open Source Repo Documentation for Coding Agents
clusters.summary: OpenWiki generates and maintains codebase documentation so coding agents can find the repo context they need without loading everything into one instruction file.

clusters.title: How to Use RLMs in Deep Agents
clusters.summary: Recursive language models (RLMs) fix context rot by having agents write code that dispatches subagents over context chunks instead of pumping everything in one context window. Deep Agents now implements this through dynamic subagents and a lightweight code interpreter, letting agents programmatically fan out work like grep, map, and reduce over large inputs. We benchmark the approach on OOLONG, a long-context reasoning task, and show it holds up where turn-by-turn agents star

clusters.title: Benchmarking Agent Tool Use
clusters.summary: Benchmark LLM tool use with 4 test environments. Compare GPT-4, Claude, and open-source models on function calling, planning, and reasoning tasks.

clusters.title: Benchmarking Single Agent Performance
clusters.summary: We explore how increasing the number of instructions and tools available to a single ReAct agent affects its performance, benchmarking models like claude-3.5-sonnet, gpt-4o, o1, and o3-mini across two domains of tasks.

clusters.title: Evaluating Skills
clusters.summary: Best practices for evaluating AI agent skills. Define tasks, measure performance, and iterate faster with LangSmith observability and evals.

clusters.title: Claude Fable 5 access restored on AI Gateway
clusters.summary: Access to Claude Fable 5, the Mythos-class model, has now been restored on AI Gateway following the US Government's decision to lift the export controls. Fable 5 is the same model that was available between June 9 and June 12. What has changed is the safety classifiers, which are now updated and more robust. In the near term, some routine tasks such as coding and debugging may trigger safety classifiers. To ensure requests are still serviced when the safety classifiers are tr

clusters.title: Enforce consistent code for agents and humans with konsistent
clusters.summary: konsistent is now open source. konsistent is a CLI linter for TypeScript codebases that enforces structural conventions, giving agents and humans the consistent context they need to implement features correctly. Deterministic, fast, and covers structural patterns that TypeScript and ESLint don't model.
